Product.description: A Product represents a digital or physical good available for purchase.

columns.name: deleted
columns.description: Whether the Product is marked as deleted. Product records are not actually deleted to maintain historical data.

columns.name: download_limit
columns.description: If digital product, how many times purchaser can download it

columns.name: download_time_limit
columns.description: Unix timestamp as integer of when download link expires if this is a digital Product

columns.name: internal_name
columns.description: name for internal use, not visible to purchasers

columns.name: level1
columns.description: The commission tier paid to level 1 Affiliates.

columns.name: level2
columns.description: The commission tier paid to level 2 Affiliates.

columns.name: offer_to_affiliates
columns.description: Whether the Product is eligible for promotion by Affiliates.

columns.name: product_type
columns.description: The Product's classification, for example 'digital' indicating it is an ebook, course, or video.

columns.name: revenue
columns.description: Ongoing total of money earned from purchases

Tag.description: A Tag is a way to label and group related objects, much like a category or segment. For example, a Tag could be assigned to Contacts when they fill out a particular Form. Tags are useful for performing actions on a subset of objects, for example sending a Message to Contacts that have been assigned a particular Tag. They are also useful for reporting by making it easy to identify groups of objects that meet a certain criteria based on the Tag(s) assigned to it.

columns.name: object_type_id
columns.description: The ID of the type of object (aka \

Message.description: A Message represents an Email that is sent as part of a Blast (one-off send), a Sequence (autoresponder), or both. There will be ObjectLogEntry records for each Contact that the Message is sent to that represent each event the Contact made tied to the Message. Each ObjectLogEntry has many ObjectLogEntryItem records associated with it that further breakdown details about each event for the Contact. Altogether, it is possible to determine any number of metrics about the Message by analyzing the data available on the Message record itself, and its associated ObjectLogEntry records and their associated ObjectLogEntryItem records.

columns.name: autosave
columns.description: The content of the last autosave.

columns.name: clicked_count
columns.description: The number of contacts that have clicked a link in the email body.

columns.name: complaints_count
columns.description: The number of times a contact marked the message as SPAM in their inbox, signaling a complaint.

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Unix timestamp as integer indicating when the message was created.

columns.name: from
columns.description: Who the message will be sent from. Options are `owner` , `custom` , or the desired user ID.

columns.name: json_data
columns.description: The content of an ONTRAmail message.

columns.name: last_auto
columns.description: Unix timestamp as integer indicating the last time the message was automatically saved.

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: Unix timestamp as integer indicating when the message was last modified.

columns.name: last_save
columns.description: Unix timestamp as integer indicating the last time the message was deliberately saved.

columns.name: message_body
columns.description: For legacy emails only, the HTML content of the message.

columns.name: not_clicked_count
columns.description: The number of contacts that have not clicked a link in the email.

columns.name: not_opened_count
columns.description: The number of contacts that have not opened the message.

columns.name: object_type_id
columns.description: The ID of the type of object (aka \

columns.name: opened_count
columns.description: The number of contacts that have opened the message.

columns.name: plaintext
columns.description: For email messages, the plain text version of your email. For SMS messages, the content to be sent.

columns.name: resource
columns.description: A JSON Object with properties that represent the full data of the message, include its status, blocks, and more.

columns.name: send_from_email
columns.description: The email address from which the message was sent. The email address set as the account default is used unless a custom address is provided via this field.

columns.name: send_out_name
columns.description: If the `send_from_email` field is set to `custom` , the name the message should come from.

columns.name: send_to
columns.description: Used only with custom objects and SMS messages. In custom objects, you can use this field to send to another email in a parent/child relationship. For example, you could send to a related child object instead of the contact's default email address. The field is stored in the format `{related_field}//{child_field}` , where `related_field` is the parent field relating one object to another and `child_field` is the actual email field in the related object you would like to send to. For SMS messages, this field defaults to `sms_number` .

columns.name: sent_count
columns.description: The number of contacts the message was sent to.

columns.name: spam_score
columns.description: The SpamAssassin spam score of the message. The lower the score, the better the rating. Messages with a spam score of 5 or higher will not be sent.

columns.name: transactional_email
columns.description: A binary integer flag indicating whether or not this message is transactional email.

columns.name: unsubscribed_count
columns.description: The number of contacts that clicked the unsubscribe link in the message to opt-out of future messages.

columns.name: utm_tracking
columns.description: A binary flag indicating whether or not the message contains UTM tracking information.

columns.name: object_type_id
columns.description: The ID of the type of object (aka \

columns.name: state
columns.type: ISO 3166-2 code as string without the country prefix. Ex: US-FL = FL
columns.shortType: ISO 3166-2 code as string without the country prefix. Ex: US-FL = FL
columns.description: State/region ISO 3166-2 code without the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 prefix. Ex: US-FL would be FL

Invoice.description: An Invoice (also known as a Transaction) represents the collection of funds to satisfy the amount owed for one or more Purchase records.

columns.name: hidden
columns.description: Whether the transaction is hidden, which is similar to being deleted except that it still remains for historical reference.

columns.name: offer_data
columns.description: JSON encoded string containing all info about products, quantities, coupons, tax, and shipping

columns.name: recharge
columns.description: Whether an additional attempt should be made to charge (process) the transaction.

columns.name: template_id
columns.description: The ID of the invoice template sent to the contact.

Subscription.description: A Subscription represents a recurring Purchase, which includes a trial period, a payment plan, or a subscription. It represents future purchases which have not been realized yet. This is different from an Order, which represents future invoices containing a transaction.

columns.name: count
columns.description: number of units from last payment date to next payment date

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Unix timestamp as integer indicating creation date (aka signup date)

columns.name: deleted
columns.description: true if order has been paid, cancelled, or deleted

columns.name: length_of_subscription_days
columns.description: number of days the open order has elapsed

columns.name: orig_month_date
columns.description: Unix timestamp as integer indicating when the first automated billing for the open order occurred, if the unit of the order is monthly.

columns.name: payments_made
columns.description: count of payments made, if recurring

columns.name: unit
columns.description: The unit of the open order. Can be daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or yearly.

Purchase.description: A Purchase represents the completion of a purchase of a Product.

columns.name: coupon_code
columns.description: code from CouponCode record, if used

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Unix timestamp as integer indicating when the purchase was made.

columns.name: level1_commission_amount
columns.description: The commission earned by the Affiliate directly associated with the purchase.

columns.name: level2_commission_amount
columns.description: The commission earned by the original Affiliate associated with the purchase in a two-tier partner program.

columns.name: package_id
columns.description: The ID of the package the purchase belongs to, if any.

columns.name: price
columns.description: product price before discounts, shipping, etc.
